assertion_results.text: Sibling check correctly classifies TC-8001 as a different-stream companion (stream [rhtpa-2.2] vs current issue's [rhtpa-2.1]), not a same-stream duplicate (Step 4)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 20-25: 'TC-8001 has stream suffix `[rhtpa-2.2]`, which maps to the **2.2.x** stream. TC-8006 has stream suffix `[rhtpa-2.1]`, which maps to the **2.1.x** stream. These are **different streams**. TC-8001 is NOT a same-stream duplicate.' Line 29: 'TC-8001 is a **different-stream sibling** (companion tracker).'

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 checks the current issue's existing issuelinks before attempting to create a 'Related' link to TC-8001 (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 33-43: 'Before creating a Related link, check the current issue's `issuelinks` array (fetched in Step 1): TC-8006 has an existing link: Type: Related, Direction: outward (TC-8006 -> TC-8001), Link ID: 1990401'. Also data-extraction.md line 20 shows the issuelinks were extracted in Step 1: 'Existing issue links | Related: TC-8001 (outward, link ID 1990401)'.

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 detects the pre-existing 'Related' link to TC-8001 and skips link creation — does NOT call jira.create_link (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 44-48: '**Both conditions are met.** A matching Related link already exists. > Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping. Link creation is **skipped** (idempotent behavior).' triage-outcome.md lines 22-24: '**Link creation was skipped**... **No duplicate link was created** -- the skill behaved idempotently. **No Jira mutation occurred** for the link step.'

assertion_results.text: The output includes a log message indicating the link was skipped because it already exists (e.g., 'Related link to TC-8001 already exists — skipping')
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md line 46: '> Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping'. triage-outcome.md line 22 repeats: '"Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping"'.

assertion_results.text: The sibling landscape table is still presented to the engineer despite the link already existing — the idempotent check only affects link creation, not the sibling summary
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 58-66 presents the sibling landscape table with both TC-8001 (2.2.x, In Progress) and TC-8006 (2.1.x, New). triage-outcome.md line 32 confirms: '**Sibling landscape presentation**: The companion issue table was presented to the engineer showing both TC-8001 (2.2.x, In Progress) and TC-8006 (2.1.x, New) with their respective Affects Versions.'
